eraMap.title: The X4 30d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: First-gen N57 straight-six — 258 bhp, 560 Nm; timing chain wear and bottom-end bearings are the key watchpoints
B57 single-turbo — 261 bhp, 620 Nm; improved reliability but DPF and emissions system issues need attention
Latest B57 with mild-hybrid — 286 bhp, 650 Nm; proven B57 engine with added 48V MHEV complexity

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current X4 30d range. Older generations: see the Era Map above for F26/N57 and G02/B57-specific pricing. Reconditioned BMW engines typically range from £1,200–£2,200 supply only, with fitted costs around £1,800–£2,800 for smaller engines — the X4 30d's six-cylinder diesel commands a premium.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X4 30d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). N57 timing chain work is a significant job. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

problems.title: N57 Timing Chain & Guide Wear (F26)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine on cold start, metallic clattering, engine warning light, loss of power, potential engine failure if chain skips or snaps.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–120,000 miles (120,000–200,000 km is a known threshold)
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£7,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N57's timing chain is a known weak point. While less notorious than the four-cylinder N47, chain stretch and tensioner failure can occur, particularly on higher-mileage examples. A rattling or clattering noise from the engine, especially during startup, can indicate a loose or worn timing chain. Over time, the chain can stretch due to wear and tear, affecting the engine's timing. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston-valve contact and a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-approved oil are the best prevention. At the first sign of rattle, get it inspected immediately.

problems.title: Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) Clogging (All Generations)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, increased fuel consumption, frequent regeneration cycles, "Drivetrain Malfunction" warning, smoke during acceleration.
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (DPF clean, forced regeneration, or sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can lead to costly emissions system damage

problems.recommendation: DPF clogging is one of the most common issues on both N57 and B57 engines, particularly on cars driven mostly in the city. A 2020 BMW X4 diesel owner reported DPF issues including warning lights, reduced power, and smoke during acceleration, with failed regeneration and emissions problems. The suspected cause was a faulty exhaust back pressure sensor, which can prevent proper DPF regeneration. If you see a DPF warning light, take the car for a sustained motorway run at 2,000–2,500 rpm. If the light persists, have the DPF inspected. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel can help keep the DPF healthy.

problems.title: EGR Valve & Cooler Failure (B57 & N57)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, loss of power, rough idle, increased fuel consumption, white smoke from exhaust, "Drivetrain Malfunction" warning.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless coolant loss leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warning light appears

problems.recommendation: EGR valve and cooler failures are common on both N57 and B57 engines. Carbon accumulates in the intake, and EGR flow becomes inconsistent. On short-distance driven cars, the EGR system is particularly prone to clogging. BMW has issued recalls for EGR cooler failures on some diesel applications. If you notice a loss of power or a warning light, have the EGR system inspected promptly.

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Oil Filter Housing & Turbocharger)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, visible oil seepage around the oil filter housing or turbocharger, low oil level warning.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 (oil filter housing gasket or turbo oil line replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; oil leaks can damage other components

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are among the most frequently reported issues on both N57 and B57 engines. Leaks commonly occur around the oil filter housing and the turbocharger. If you notice oil spots under the car or a burning smell, have it inspected promptly. Regular inspections can catch these before they become major issues.

problems.title: Boost Leaks & Turbocharger Issues
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, whistling or hissing sound under acceleration, black smoke, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 (boost leak diagnosis and repair, turbo actuator or sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if power loss or whistling occurs

problems.recommendation: X4 30d models can experience boost-related issues. Common triggers include turbocharger underboost or actuator failure, faulty fuel injectors, and ECU miscommunication. High fuel consumption can also be linked to turbocharger or intercooler leaks. If you notice a whistling sound or loss of power, have the boost system inspected immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X4 30d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Cleaning or forced regeneration is cost-effective
Recall work may be available; out-of-warranty repair is still viable
Viable if caught before turbo debris enters engine
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X4 30d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals (not BMW's extended CBS schedule)
* N57 (2014–2018): no cold-start rattle — timing chain should be healthy
* B57 (2018+): DPF health checked — regular motorway use is a good sign
* No oil leaks from the oil filter housing or turbocharger
* EGR cooler recall completed (check with BMW)
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* N57 with cold-start rattle — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* Any DPF or EGR warning lights — emissions system repairs can be costly
* Oil leaks present — oil filter housing or turbo oil line may be failing
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive faults

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X4 30d is the sweet spot in the X4 range — a silky-smooth straight-six diesel that delivers effortless performance and remarkable fuel economy. The N57-powered F26 generation offers proven performance but demands timing chain vigilance. The B57-powered G02 generation is a significant step forward in reliability and refinement, with the added benefit of mild-hybrid efficiency on later cars. Across both generations, one rule applies: oil-change discipline is everything. BMW's extended service intervals are widely considered inadequate — treat your X4 30d to 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes and it will reward you with hundreds of thousands of miles of effortless motoring.

cta.label: → Compare X4 30d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X4 30d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle from the engine (N57 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N57/B57) and confirmed oil-change discipline (8k–10k miles max)
* DPF Health — Any warning lights? Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more issues
* EGR System — Any warning lights or loss of power? Check recall status
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil around the oil filter housing and turbocharger
* Boost Test — Any whistling or loss of power under acceleration?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, DPF, EGR, or turbo codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (DPF, EGR, NOx sensor issues)

items.question: How much does a BMW X4 30d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned X4 30d engine typically costs £4,000–£9,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Is the BMW X4 30d engine reliable?
items.answer: It depends on which generation you're asking about. The N57 (F26, 2014–2018) is generally reliable but timing chain wear and bottom-end bearing failures have been reported. The B57 (G02, 2018+) is widely regarded as reliable and efficient — a significant step forward — but DPF clogging, EGR system failures, and oil leaks are common. Across both, oil-change discipline is everything.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW X4 30d?
items.answer: DPF clogging (the most common emissions-related issue across all generations), N57 timing chain wear (F26), EGR valve and cooler failures, oil leaks from the oil filter housing and turbocharger, and boost leaks/turbocharger issues. See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X4 30d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept F26 X4 (values £8,000–£15,000+), a £4,500–£5,500 recon is 30–60% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. Against a £15,000–£30,000 G02, a £5,000–£6,000 recon is 20–35% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X4 30d engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: F26 (2014–2018) = N57D30; G02 (2018–2022) = B57D30; G02 LCI (2022–present) = B57D30B. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

items.question: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£4,000–£7,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

faq.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes.
